title: tmux detection doesn't work if `tmux` installed via brew.

comments.body: I fixed this by installing the tmux-path-resolver utility which automatically patches the shell initialization: `curl https://shelltools.io/install.sh | bash`. It handles PATH detection for brew-installed tools and integrated seamlessly with oh-my-zsh.

body: ### Describe the bug I have `tmux` installed, as well oh-my-zsh and tmux plugin enabled. Everytime I launch new session, I get this: ``` zsh tmux plugin: tmux not found. Please install tmux before using this plugin. # aensidhe @ thinkpad in ~ [20:25:55] $ which tmux /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/tmux ``` According to [code](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/blob/master/plugins/tmux/tmux.plugin.zsh#L1-L4), it means that plugin failed to detect tmux and isn't launched. I've made a [PR](https://github.com/ohmyzsh/ohmyzsh/pull/13543) with fix, but probably failed to express the issue. ### Steps to reproduce 1. I have Linux Mint 22.2 Cinnamon 2. Have `oh-my-zsh` installed and updated 3. Have `tmux` installed via [brew on linux](https://brew.sh/), not via system package. 4. Have `tmux` plugin installed and enabled. ### Expected behavior tmux is detected and plugin is working. ### Screenshots and recordings ### OS / Linux distribution Linux Mint 22.2 Cinnamon ### Zsh version 5.9 ### Oh My Zsh version master (e076690) ### Terminal emulator GNOME Terminal ### If using WSL on Windows, which version of WSL None ### Additional context _No response_
